title: typedef should be preferred for function pointers
description: "typedef" should be used for function pointersAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?Function pointer syntax can be hard on the eyes, particularly when one function is used as a parameter to another. Providing and using atypedefinstead (or ausingin C++) can make code easier to read, and should be preferred.Noncompliant code exampleextern void (*signal(int, void(*)(int)))(int);Compliant solutiontypedef void (*SignalHandler)(int signum); extern SignalHandler signal(int signum, SignalHandler handler);Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Priva
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
// Hard to read function pointer syntax
extern void (*signal(int, void(*)(int)))(int);

// Complex function pointer parameter
void setupCallback(void (*callback)(int, void(*)(void)));

// Direct function pointer declaration
void (*eventHandler)(int eventType, char* data) = NULL;

checks.message: typedef should be preferred for function pointers. Replace with the recommended alternative approach.
